title: Chipmaker TSMC sees 36% growth in sales, 68% surge in June revenues amid sustained global demand for AI hardware
description: The Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) today reported 36% rise in quarterly sales and 68% in June revenues amid sustained global demand for artificial intelligence (AI) hardware.

title: based cyberattacks replacing traditional hacking as biggest threat to financial institutions: Report
description: Cyber threats targeting financial institutions are rapidly shifting from traditional hacking to trust-based attacks, with cybercriminals increasingly exploiting digital identities, artificial intelligence (AI), payment systems and third-party ecosystems instead of directly targeting passwords or transactions, according to the Digital Threat Report 2025-26

title: Oil surges as another AI company makes solid sharemarket debut
description: A flat finish for the Australian sharemarket masked a volatile trading session, with the ASX spending most of the day in negative territory as Brent crude oil jumped 5 per cent on renewed uncertainty in the Middle East. The session also followed the blockbuster US debut of another AI-related company, raising fresh questions about where the next investment opportunities in artificial intelligence could emerge. SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Ben Lam, Head of Equities at Colonial First State, about the outlook for AI investing and the day’s market moves.

title: million dollar deal to transform global network operations with AI
description: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and ABB signed a multi-million, multi-year agreement to transform the technology leader's global network operations using artificial intelligence. The expanded collaboration marks the next phase of a 20-year partnership between the two entities.

title: Mulling AI investment, Anthropic lobbied Australia on copyright law
description: Anthropic's chief executive Dario Amodei has lobbied Australian officials for "copyright reform" as the artificial intelligence giant seeks to make a major investment in the country, official briefing notes released Monday show. According to briefing notes released under freedom of information law, Amodei had requested the meeting to discuss barriers to AI training in Australia, "particularly copyright reform".
